title: Biome Components
description: Any components that this Biome uses

minecraft:climate.title: Minecraft Climate
minecraft:climate.description: Describes temperature, humidity, precipitation, and similar. Biomes without this component will have default values.

defaultSnippets.label: Biome Climate
defaultSnippets.description: Describes temperature, humidity, precipitation, and similar climate properties for a biome. Biomes without this component will use default values. Temperature affects visual effects like snow/ice placement, sponge drying, and sky color. Downfall controls precipitation frequency and intensity.

minecraft:creature_spawn_probability.title: Minecraft Creature Spawn Probability
minecraft:creature_spawn_probability.description: Probability that creatures will spawn within the biome when a chunk is generated.

defaultSnippets.label: Biome Creature Spawn Probability
defaultSnippets.description: Probability that creatures will spawn within the biome when a chunk is generated.

minecraft:humidity.title: Minecraft Humidity
minecraft:humidity.description: Forces a biome to ether always be humid or never humid. Humidity effects the spread chance, and spread rate of fire in the biome

defaultSnippets.label: Biome Humidity
defaultSnippets.description: Forces a biome to ether always be humid or never humid. Humidity effects the spread chance, and spread rate of fire in the biome

minecraft:map_tints.title: Minecraft Map Tints
minecraft:map_tints.description: Sets the color grass and foliage will be tinted by in this biome on the map.

defaultSnippets.label: Biome Map Tints
defaultSnippets.description: Sets the color grass and foliage will be tinted by in this biome on the map.

minecraft:mountain_parameters.title: Minecraft Mountain Parameters
minecraft:mountain_parameters.description: Noise parameters used to drive mountain terrain generation in Overworld.

defaultSnippets.label: Biome Mountain Parameters
defaultSnippets.description: Noise parameters used to drive mountain terrain generation in Overworld.

minecraft:multinoise_generation_rules.title: Minecraft Multinoise Generation Rules
minecraft:multinoise_generation_rules.description: Controls how this biome is instantiated (and then potentially modified) during world generation of the nether.

defaultSnippets.label: Biome Multinoise Generation Rules
defaultSnippets.description: Controls how this biome is instantiated (and then potentially modified) during world generation of the nether.

minecraft:overworld_generation_rules.title: Minecraft Overworld Generation Rules
minecraft:overworld_generation_rules.description: Controls how this biome is instantiated (and then potentially modified) during world generation of the overworld.

minecraft:overworld_height.title: Minecraft Overworld Height
minecraft:overworld_height.description: Noise parameters used to drive terrain height in the Overworld.

defaultSnippets.label: Biome Overworld Height
defaultSnippets.description: Noise parameters used to drive terrain height in the Overworld.

minecraft:partially_frozen.title: Minecraft Partially Frozen
minecraft:partially_frozen.description: Component will impact the temperature in a frozen biome, causing some areas to not be frozen. Ex: patchy ice, patchy snow

minecraft:replace_biomes.title: Minecraft Replace Biomes
minecraft:replace_biomes.description: Replaces a specified portion of one or more Minecraft biomes.

defaultSnippets.label: Biome Replace Biomes
defaultSnippets.description: Replaces a specified portion of one or more Minecraft biomes.

minecraft:subsurface_builder.title: Minecraft Subsurface Builder
minecraft:subsurface_builder.description: Sub Surface Builders allow specifying a `minecraft:surface_builder` to be applied to biomes located underneath regular terrain surface. Note, however, that pre-existing surface builder types' processing have not been updated to accommodate the ability to specify them for sub-terrain height ranges, which may lead to unexpected results when using them.

defaultSnippets.label: Biome Surface Builder
defaultSnippets.description: Controls materials used for terrain generation.

minecraft:surface_builder.title: Minecraft Surface Builder
minecraft:surface_builder.description: Controls materials used for terrain generation.

defaultSnippets.label: Biome Surface Builder
defaultSnippets.description: Controls materials used for terrain generation.

minecraft:surface_material_adjustments.title: Minecraft Surface Material Adjustments
minecraft:surface_material_adjustments.description: Specify fine-detail changes to blocks used in terrain generation (based on a noise function).

minecraft:tags.title: Minecraft Tags
minecraft:tags.description:
Attach arbitrary string tags to this biome.
Most biome tags are referenced by JSON settings, but some meanings of tags are directly implemented in the game's code. These tags are listed here:
birch: Biome uses wildflowers (mutually exclusive with other flower biome tags). Does nothing if biome is tagged "hills".
cold: Villagers will be dressed for snowy weather.
deep: Pre-Caves and Cliffs, prevents an ocean from having islands or connected rivers and makes the biome less likely to have hills.
desert: Allows partially-buried ruined portals to be placed in the biome. Sand blocks will play ambient sounds when the player is nearby.
extreme_hills: Ruined portals can be placed higher than normal. Biomes tagged "forest" or "forest_generation" will use normal Overworld flowers instead of forest flowers.
flower_forest: Biome uses forest flowers (mutually exclusive with other flower biome tags).
forest: Biome uses forest flowers (mutually exclusive with other flower biome tags). Does nothing if biome is tagged tagged "taiga" or "extreme_hills".
forest_generation: Equivalent to "forest".
frozen: Villagers will be dressed for snowy weather. Prevents the biome from containing lava springs if it is also tagged "ocean".
ice: Around ruined portals, lava is always replaced by Netherrack and Netherrack cannot be replaced by magma.
ice_plains: Prevents the biome from containing lava springs if it is also tagged "mutated".
jungle: Ruined portals will be very mossy.
hills: Biomes tagged "meadow" or "birch" will use normal Overworld flowers instead of wildflowers.
meadow: Biome uses wildflowers (mutually exclusive with other flower biome tags). Does nothing if biome is tagged "hills".
mesa: Sand blocks will play ambient sounds when the player is nearby.
mountain: Ruined portals can be placed higher than normal.
mutated: Pre-Caves and Cliffs, prevents switching to the specified "mutate_transformation" as the biome is already considered mutated. Prevents the biome from containing lava springs if it is also tagged "ice_plains".
no_legacy_worldgen: Prevents biome from using legacy world generation behavior unless the biome is being placed in the Overworld.
ocean: Prevents the biome from containing lava springs if it is also tagged "frozen". Allows ruined portals to be found underwater. Pre-Caves and Cliffs, determines if shorelines and rivers should be placed at the edges of the biome and identifies the biome as a shallow ocean for placing islands, unless the "deep" tag is present.
pale_garden: Biome uses closed-eye blossoms (mutually exclusive with other flower biome tags).
plains: Biome uses plains flowers (mutually exclusive with other flower biome tags).
rare: Pre-Caves and Cliffs, this tag flags the biome as a special biome. Oceans cannot be special.
swamp: Allows ruined portals to be found underwater. Biome uses swamp flowers (mutually exclusive with other flower biome tags).
taiga: Biomes tagged "forest" or "forest_generation" will use normal Overworld flowers instead of forest flowers.

minecraft:village_type.title: Minecraft Village Type
minecraft:village_type.description: Determines the type of village for the Biome

defaultSnippets.label: Biome Village Type
defaultSnippets.description: Determines the type of village for the Biome
